en: The Thai writing system includes 44 consonant letters.
These consonants are essential for reading Thai words and determining tone classes.
The Thai alphabet contains 44 consonant letters.
Each consonant traditionally has a name associated with a word.
Thai consonants belong to three tone classes: high, middle, and low.
These classes help determine the tone of a syllable.
Thai consonants appear at the beginning and end of syllables.
Learning them is the first step to reading Thai.
Some Thai consonants look very similar, especially for beginners.
Practising recognition regularly helps avoid confusion.
